Title: Toward Developing a Yeast Cell Factory for the Production of Prenylated Flavonoids
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoestrogen with estrogenic, antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anticancer activities
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Plasmid-driven expression in engineered yeast strains
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A truncated S. cerevisiae HMG1 was coexpressed with SfFPT to increase prenyl donor availability. 2. The study presents tHMG1 as a key mevalonate-pathway support module expected to raise DMAPP supply for prenylation. 3. In strains carrying pMEN2, de novo 8-prenylnaringenin production was achieved, although DMAPP supply likely remained limiting.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports 8-prenylnaringenin production by increasing prenyl-donor supply capacity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Plasmid overexpression in yeast; metabolite profiling by UHPLC-MS
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Shake flask cultivation on minimal medium with glucose; some assays with naringenin supplementation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression of cytosolic HMG-CoA reductase is associated with squalene accumulation in yeast; DMAPP still likely limiting
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a support module to boost prenyl-donor supply in microbial engineering of prenylated flavonoids, likely in combination with additional mevalonate-pathway tuning
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included on episomal plasmid pMEN2 together with coSfFPT; described as key regulatory enzyme of the mevalonate pathway

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoestrogen with estrogenic, antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anticancer activities
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Integrated replacement of yeast TSC13 coding sequence
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Replacement of endogenous TSC13 with codon-optimized MdECR eliminated phloretic acid byproduct formation. 2. This rerouted flux to increase naringenin production. 3. In combination with SfFPT expression, higher precursor supply contributed to improved 8-prenylnaringenin output.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indirectly increases 8-prenylnaringenin by preventing precursor drain to phloretic acid and enhancing naringenin accumulation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 gene replacement; metabolite profiling by UHPLC-MS
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Shake flask cultivation on minimal medium with glucose
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful flux-redirection module to suppress phloretic acid side-product formation in yeast flavonoid production platforms
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts here as a replacement for endogenous yeast TSC13 rather than as the focal catalytic step to the final prenylated product

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoestrogen with estrogenic, antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anticancer activities
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Integrated in engineered yeast strain PATW103
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. coTAL1 introduction was used to draw from the yeast tyrosine pool for flavonoid precursor formation. 2. In the enhanced precursor background, naringenin production rose to 100 mg/L in PATW103-derived strain PPF6. 3. This increased precursor supply was associated with the highest 8-prenylnaringenin titer observed.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indirectly promotes 8-prenylnaringenin accumulation by increasing upstream naringenin precursor supply
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome integration; strain comparison; metabolite profiling by UHPLC-MS
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Shake flask cultivation on minimal medium with glucose
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply engineering module for yeast production of naringenin-derived nutraceutical flavonoids
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its effect is indirect through precursor pathway enhancement rather than direct prenylation of naringenin

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a dimethylallyl prenyl group to naringenin at the C-8 position to form 8-prenylnaringenin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant; prenylation efficiency remained a major bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predicted outer membrane/plastid-type membrane protein; discussion suggests ER retargeting may improve performance in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent phytoestrogen with estrogenic, antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anticancer activities
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mostly culture medium, trace in cell pellet
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Plasmid-driven codon-optimized expression in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Introduction of codon-optimized SfFPT into naringenin-producing S. cerevisiae enabled de novo production of 8-prenylnaringenin from glucose. 2. 8-Prenylnaringenin was detected in PPF4 culture medium but not in control strains. 3. Naringenin feeding increased 8-prenylnaringenin production about 12-fold, showing substrate supply limits flux through SfFPT. 4. In the best engineered background (PPF6), 8-prenylnaringenin reached 0.12 mg/L (0.35 μM).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in yeast; comparison with control strain; naringenin feeding test; UHPLC-MS product detection and quantification; MS2 confirmation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Shake flask cultivation on minimal medium with glucose; optional supplementation with 250 μM naringenin
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low conversion of naringenin to 8-prenylnaringenin indicates remaining bottlenecks, likely limited DMAPP supply and/or subcellular mismatch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core catalytic gene for microbial production of 8-prenylnaringenin and a platform enzyme for discovery/engineering of other flavonoid prenyltransferases
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly enables and increases 8-prenylnaringenin production in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chosen over SfN8DT-1 because reported to have higher affinity and catalytic efficiency with naringenin and DMAPP

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Tyrosine-derived entry to p-coumarate/naringenin precursor branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-tyrosine to form p-coumaric acid, increasing precursor supply for naringenin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoestrogen with estrogenic, antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anticancer activities
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Introduction of coTAL1 was intended to exploit the yeast tyrosine pool for naringenin biosynthesis. 2. In the engineered background, naringenin production reached 100 mg/L. 3. The increased precursor supply coincided with the highest 8-prenylnaringenin titer observed.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome integration; strain comparison; UHPLC-MS metabolite quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Shake flask cultivation on minimal medium with glucose
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves precursor supply to boost final 8-prenylnaringenin production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although upstream to the final product, it is an enzyme gene directly used in the experimental engineering strategy
